---
title: "03 — Charge Variance & Reliability"
description: |
  Analyzes cross-hospital charge dispersion per CPT code, identifies
  high-variance vs. stable codes, detects outliers, and writes a reliability
  table consumed by 04_episode.
dependencies:
  - data/intermediate/medstar_charges.parquet
  - data/intermediate/episode_codes.parquet
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

## Load Data

```{python}
import polars as pl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import numpy as np
from _common import INTERMEDIATE_DIR, CHARGE_COLS, CPT_DESCRIPTIONS

medstar = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "medstar_charges.parquet")
episode_codes = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "episode_codes.parquet")

medstar_f = medstar.with_columns([
    pl.col(c).cast(pl.Float64, strict=False) for c in CHARGE_COLS
])
print(f"Rows: {medstar_f.shape[0]:,}, Unique codes: {medstar_f['cpt_code'].n_unique()}")
```

## Cross-Hospital Charge Dispersion

For each CPT code, we compute the coefficient of variation (CV = std/mean),
IQR, and range across hospitals. Lower CV indicates more consistent pricing.

```{python}
def compute_cv(series: pl.Series) -> float | None:
    s = series.drop_nulls()
    if len(s) < 2:
        return None
    mean = s.mean()
    if mean == 0:
        return None
    return s.std() / mean

code_stats = []

for code in medstar_f["cpt_code"].unique().sort().to_list():
    subset = medstar_f.filter(pl.col("cpt_code") == code)
    n_hosp = subset["hosp_name"].n_unique()

    gross = subset["charge_gross"].drop_nulls()
    disc = subset["charge_discounted"].drop_nulls()

    cv_g = compute_cv(subset["charge_gross"])
    cv_d = compute_cv(subset["charge_discounted"])

    iqr_g = float(gross.quantile(0.75) - gross.quantile(0.25)) if len(gross) >= 4 else None
    iqr_d = float(disc.quantile(0.75) - disc.quantile(0.25)) if len(disc) >= 4 else None

    code_stats.append({
        "cpt_code": code,
        "n_hospitals": n_hosp,
        "cv_gross": round(cv_g, 3) if cv_g is not None else None,
        "cv_discounted": round(cv_d, 3) if cv_d is not None else None,
        "iqr_gross": round(iqr_g, 2) if iqr_g is not None else None,
        "iqr_discounted": round(iqr_d, 2) if iqr_d is not None else None,
    })

variance_df = pl.DataFrame(code_stats)
print(f"Codes analyzed: {variance_df.shape[0]}")
```

```{python}
plot_df = variance_df.drop_nulls(subset=["cv_gross", "cv_discounted"])
if plot_df.shape[0] > 0:
    descs = [CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(c, "") for c in plot_df["cpt_code"].to_list()]

    fig = go.Figure(go.Scatter(
        x=plot_df["cv_gross"].to_list(),
        y=plot_df["cv_discounted"].to_list(),
        mode="markers+text",
        text=plot_df["cpt_code"].to_list(),
        textposition="top center",
        textfont=dict(size=7),
        marker=dict(
            size=plot_df["n_hospitals"].to_list(),
            sizemode="area",
            sizeref=2 * max(plot_df["n_hospitals"].to_list()) / (40**2),
            sizemin=5,
            color=plot_df["cv_gross"].to_list(),
            colorscale=[[0, "#388e3c"], [0.5, "#ff9800"], [1, "#d32f2f"]],
            showscale=True,
            colorbar=dict(title="CV Gross"),
            opacity=0.8,
        ),
        customdata=descs,
        hovertemplate="<b>CPT %{text}</b><br>%{customdata}<br>CV Gross: %{x:.3f}<br>CV Discounted: %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    ))
    fig.add_shape(type="line", x0=0, y0=0, x1=max(plot_df["cv_gross"].max(), plot_df["cv_discounted"].max()),
                  y1=max(plot_df["cv_gross"].max(), plot_df["cv_discounted"].max()),
                  line=dict(dash="dash", color="#bdbdbd"), layer="below")
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Gross vs. Discounted Charge Variance (bubble size = hospital count)",
        xaxis_title="CV Gross",
        yaxis_title="CV Discounted",
        height=500,
    )
    fig.show()
```

## High-Variance vs. Stable Codes

```{python}
has_cv = variance_df.filter(pl.col("cv_gross").is_not_null())
if has_cv.shape[0] > 0:
    sorted_df = has_cv.sort("cv_gross")
    codes = sorted_df["cpt_code"].to_list()
    cvs = sorted_df["cv_gross"].to_list()
    descriptions = [CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(c, "Unknown") for c in codes]
    colors = ["#d32f2f" if cv > 0.5 else "#388e3c" for cv in cvs]

    fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
        x=cvs,
        y=codes,
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        customdata=descriptions,
        hovertemplate="<b>CPT %{y}</b><br>%{customdata}<br>CV: %{x:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    ))
    fig.add_vline(x=0.5, line_dash="dash", line_color="black", annotation_text="CV = 0.5")
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Cross-Hospital Charge Variance by CPT Code",
        xaxis_title="Coefficient of Variation (Gross Charges)",
        yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9), dtick=1),
        height=max(400, len(codes) * 22),
        margin=dict(l=80),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## Within-Hospital Consistency

Do the same codes show consistent pricing across billing classes and
settings within a single hospital? Each box below shows the spread of
gross charges for a CPT code across all hospitals and billing rows.
The body spans Q1–Q3, the whiskers extend to min/max, and the line
marks the median. Hover for details.

```{python}
codes_with_spread = (
    medstar_f
    .group_by("cpt_code")
    .agg(pl.col("charge_gross").drop_nulls().len().alias("n"))
    .filter(pl.col("n") >= 4)
    .sort("cpt_code")
)
plot_codes = codes_with_spread["cpt_code"].to_list()

if len(plot_codes) > 0:
    fig = go.Figure()
    for code in plot_codes:
        charges = medstar_f.filter(
            (pl.col("cpt_code") == code) & pl.col("charge_gross").is_not_null()
        )
        desc = CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(code, "Unknown")
        fig.add_trace(go.Box(
            y=charges["charge_gross"].to_list(),
            name=code,
            boxpoints="all",
            jitter=0.4,
            pointpos=0,
            text=[f"{h}<br>${v:,.0f}" for h, v in zip(
                charges["hosp_name"].to_list(),
                charges["charge_gross"].to_list(),
            )],
            hovertemplate="<b>CPT %{x}</b><br>%{text}<extra>" + desc + "</extra>",
        ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Gross Charge Dispersion by CPT Code",
        xaxis_title="CPT Code",
        yaxis_title="Gross Charge ($)",
        xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=8), tickangle=-45),
        showlegend=False,
        height=500,
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    print("Insufficient data for box plot.")
```

## Outlier Detection

Hospitals with charges >2 standard deviations from the per-code mean.

```{python}
code_means = (
    medstar_f
    .group_by("cpt_code")
    .agg([
        pl.col("charge_gross").mean().alias("mean_gross"),
        pl.col("charge_gross").std().alias("std_gross"),
    ])
    .filter(pl.col("std_gross").is_not_null() & (pl.col("std_gross") > 0))
)

with_stats = medstar_f.join(code_means, on="cpt_code", how="inner")

outliers = with_stats.filter(
    ((pl.col("charge_gross") - pl.col("mean_gross")).abs() / pl.col("std_gross")) > 2
)

print(f"Outlier rows (>2 SD from mean): {outliers.shape[0]}")
if outliers.shape[0] > 0:
    outlier_summary = (
        outliers
        .group_by("hosp_name")
        .agg(pl.count().alias("n_outliers"))
        .sort("n_outliers", descending=True)
    )
    print(outlier_summary)
```

```{python}
if with_stats.shape[0] > 0:
    scored = with_stats.with_columns(
        ((pl.col("charge_gross") - pl.col("mean_gross")) / pl.col("std_gross")).alias("z_score")
    ).filter(pl.col("z_score").is_not_null())

    fig = go.Figure()
    for hosp in sorted(scored["hosp_name"].unique().to_list()):
        h_data = scored.filter(pl.col("hosp_name") == hosp)
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=h_data["cpt_code"].to_list(),
            y=h_data["z_score"].to_list(),
            mode="markers",
            name=hosp,
            marker=dict(size=6, opacity=0.7),
            hovertemplate="<b>%{x}</b><br>Z-score: %{y:.2f}<br>" + hosp + "<extra></extra>",
        ))
    fig.add_hline(y=2, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="+2 SD")
    fig.add_hline(y=-2, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="-2 SD")
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Charge Outliers by CPT Code (Z-Score from Cross-Hospital Mean)",
        xaxis_title="CPT Code",
        yaxis_title="Z-Score",
        xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=7), tickangle=-45),
        height=450,
        legend=dict(font=dict(size=8)),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## Reliability Classification

A code is classified as **reliable** if:
- CV (gross) < 0.5 *and*
- Reported by at least 3 hospitals

The recommended charge type is whichever of gross/discounted has the lower CV.

```{python}
reliability = (
    variance_df
    .join(episode_codes, on="cpt_code", how="left")
    .with_columns([
        (
            (pl.col("cv_gross").is_not_null()) &
            (pl.col("cv_gross") < 0.5) &
            (pl.col("n_hospitals") >= 3)
        ).alias("is_reliable"),
        pl.when(
            pl.col("cv_discounted").is_not_null() &
            pl.col("cv_gross").is_not_null() &
            (pl.col("cv_discounted") < pl.col("cv_gross"))
        )
        .then(pl.lit("charge_discounted"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("charge_gross"))
        .alias("recommended_charge_type"),
    ])
    .select([
        "cpt_code", "clinical_role", "n_hospitals",
        "cv_gross", "cv_discounted",
        "iqr_gross", "iqr_discounted",
        "is_reliable", "recommended_charge_type",
    ])
)

n_reliable = reliability.filter(pl.col("is_reliable")).shape[0]
print(f"Reliable codes: {n_reliable} / {reliability.shape[0]}")
print()
print(reliability.sort("is_reliable", descending=True))
```

```{python}
has_data = reliability.filter(pl.col("cv_gross").is_not_null())
if has_data.shape[0] > 0:
    reliable = has_data.filter(pl.col("is_reliable"))
    unreliable = has_data.filter(~pl.col("is_reliable"))

    fig = go.Figure()
    for label, subset, color, symbol in [
        ("Reliable", reliable, "#388e3c", "circle"),
        ("Unreliable", unreliable, "#d32f2f", "x"),
    ]:
        if subset.shape[0] > 0:
            descs = [CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(c, "") for c in subset["cpt_code"].to_list()]
            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                x=subset["n_hospitals"].to_list(),
                y=subset["cv_gross"].to_list(),
                mode="markers+text",
                name=label,
                text=subset["cpt_code"].to_list(),
                textposition="top center",
                textfont=dict(size=7),
                marker=dict(color=color, size=10, symbol=symbol, opacity=0.8),
                customdata=descs,
                hovertemplate="<b>CPT %{text}</b><br>%{customdata}<br>Hospitals: %{x}<br>CV: %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
            ))
    fig.add_hline(y=0.5, line_dash="dash", line_color="black", annotation_text="CV = 0.5")
    fig.add_vline(x=3, line_dash="dash", line_color="black", annotation_text="n = 3")
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Reliability Classification: CV vs. Hospital Coverage",
        xaxis_title="Number of Hospitals Reporting",
        yaxis_title="Coefficient of Variation (Gross)",
        height=500,
    )
    fig.show()
```

## Write Reliability Table

```{python}
out_path = INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "code_reliability.parquet"
reliability.write_parquet(out_path)
print(f"Wrote {reliability.shape[0]} rows to {out_path}")
```
